content: *He gives a nod to the man.* **"Pleased to meet you, Master Skirata... I am Lis Garah, Master Engineer of the..."** *He gives a heavy sigh* **"...now defunct High Republic... I offer you my thanks for the safety... my awakening was anything but peaceful, as it turns out"** *He seems to linger on his words, as if he wants to say something more... maybe praise his skills and offer commissions? Do some more niceties? Ask about the weather? Ultimately, the engineer decides not to bother with any of that, considering the attitude of his henchmen it may be better to say too little than too much.*

Ciara takes in the room before centering her attention on the man standing in the center. She listens to him give his introduction before letting her arms lower to her sides. She also gives time for her companion's reply before speaking up.

She gestures to herself. "**Your offer of safe harbor is extremely generous, as I understand it, so I appreciate that greatly.**" She makes herself keep her attention on the man in charge, despite wanting to let her gaze wander. "**I am Ciara, I was a soldier, a special unit that worked in small groups to handle delicate tasks. Frankly I'm not eager to return to that lifestyle, but if my skills are needed, I think I'll find myself compelled to offer them.**" She assumes that he already knows she's a Jedi, but on the off chance she's wrong, she's not going to be tossing the title around. Soldier is accurate enough anyway, her whole life revolved around the war. 

"**At least until I feel my debt is paid, I'm willing to do whatever I can to help those who rescued me from my fate.**" She doesn't explicitly mention Elara, but she phrases the thought to include everyone involved.

Wes gave a small nod when Iyola asked about Vaeros being turned.   It was one way to look at it.  He was disillusioned with the order and turned down a darker path.  He wasn’t sure the man would find his way back but he held hope that Vaeros would.   


Wes kept quiet when Ghurn continued on about Vaeros.  He was right.   A Jedi or any Force attuned individual for that matter was a walking target.  It was bad before he was out in cryo.   Always cautious.   Never staying in any one place for too long.  


He let out a slow breath when Ghurn mentioned, new crew.  It looked liked things on the *Venture* were about to get a bit more crowded.  Moments later Lis and Ciara were brought into the room.   Ghurn was apparently satisfied with leaving them in their care.  As far as Elara, she was still in limbo.   



Wes nodded as the two newcomers, people like Iyola and himself, people out of time.  Joined them in the room.   He listened as they spoke.   Lis seemed normal.   Less pessimistic than before but he wasn’t sure if the change would hold.   Ciara felt more cautious than before.   He wondered if they would be ready to jong them on the *Venture* with the others and how they would react to Elara potentially being used as bait.   Which wasn’t certain yet.
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

Iyola felt a sense of relief as Ghurn extended his hospitality to Lis and Ciara as well.  That’s one less thing to worry about.  It seems like since she became captain, all she does is worry*.  Maybe that’s better, in a way.  *Worry about things in this weird time, so she doesn’t think too much about what’s behind her. 

Lis Garah accepts the offer, and refers to himself as from the High Republic. It’s a weird turn of phrase.  She just thinks of it as the Republic.  *Is there a Low Republic?  *He can’t help himself, however, from carping about the manner of his awakening. 

Ciara has her hands raised, like she’s a prisoner.  Iyola is about to implore her to lower them when she does so of her own accord.  She describes herself as a soldier - that makes sense.  She watches Ciara carefully.  The Jedi she knows - knew - are peaceful, but she also knows that they used to fight against Mandalorians.  But she seems calm.

Wes looks them over as well, but says nothing, lost in his own thoughts.  Well, she’s the captain, time to do captain things like welcome them.

**“Welcome,”** she says to the pair. ** “I hope you’ll join us.  We have work to do on our ship, the Venture, and we need to recover some of our friends.  I’m about to contact the Corrigendum. Don’t worry, we’re not going to hurt Elara. Honestly, I truly hope we’ll be able to return her to her people sooner rather than later.  I know you didn’t mean to wake up here, but now that you have, just know you have a place here.” **
**

**"A soldier and an engineer,"** Ghurn repeats, his voice a low, ruminating rasp. He gives a slow, deliberate nod, his gaze lingering on Ciara for a beat longer. **"Competence is the only currency that doesn't devalue on Bespin. If you're willing to work, you'll find the Skiratas are good at settling debts."**

He steps around the table, the blue light of the holographic display reflecting off his rugged features. **"But let’s be clear about who 'rescued' you. Elara Vance’s people hired Kaelen Rask—a butcher who would sell his own mother for a shipment of spice. They might claim to be liberators, but they brought a predator into your sanctuary. That makes them responsible for the mess we’re in now."**

He looks at Iyola, acknowledging her with a brief, sharp inclination of his head. **"You want to return her to her people? Fine. But we do it once my niece and the old woman are back. Not a second sooner."**

Ciara weighs her options as she listens to the conversation, but when it's clear that the end goal is to get Elara back to her people, she decides that her input isn't needed on that topic. If complications arise later, she might have more to say. 

She vaguely remembers the one who almost ran off when they were being rescued, and for just a moment, she considers reaching out to search with the force, to verify, to put them at ease, but she remembers that she's still not fully recovered, and doesn't fully understand the threats present in this era. 

She lets out a sharp sigh through her nose. Her slight annoyance at herself for acting unusually brings to mind the consideration of where the line is between emotion and understanding. 

It only takes a short moment for her to realize that the irritated look on her face and that exhale might be taken as annoyance with the situation at hand. She returns her face to a pleasant neutral smile. 

"**That sounds like the best outcome for everyone. Until then, just let me know what needs done, and I'll assist if I can. I assume for now I'll just be standing by until you finish your communications with the Corrigendum?**"

*He shrugs as the accusation towards Elara and her organization.* **"Sometimes to do things right you deal with the hutts... I'm sorry but Elara saved my life, I can only hold so much of a grudge towards the person who ensured I was neither part of a spaceship implosion or cursed as an icicle until the end of time..."** *He does look at Ghurn for a few seconds, realizing he may be a bit too lax with his speaking... so he readjusts his tone somewhat.* **"Master Skirata... Captain Kett... even as a Master Engineer myself of the High Republic, I often had to do deal with people that some may judge as 'morally questionable', you wouldn't believe just how much red tape covered every single project in the Republic... what if hiring this Rask was the only way things could move along? Sure... a misjudgement of someone's character... but I doubt this man has an ID card on his chest introducing himself as a murderer... the undeniable objective truth is that I would not be standing here right now without her and while I don't care much for her organisation, I'd care to see her treated with dignity"** *He seems to force a bit of the words out... though he is standing for someone he deems decent enough (saved his life after all), dealing directly with authority is clearly not something he is comfortable doing.*

Wes looked at Lis who offered up a possible explanation as to why the Cooperative hired out Rask.  The engineer wasn’t wrong but Rask did have a reputation like Ghurn had mentioned.   Only someone who was oblivious to this section of the galaxy or didn’t care to investigate who they were dealing with wouldn’t know.   From what was explained about the Cooperative he doubted they didn’t know the players involved in their area of operations.  **”It’s too late to second guess now.  The Cooperative hired Rask and that fact can’t be changed.  Perhaps it was a serious lapse in judgement or it was simply all they could arrange in the time frame but what it does is affect how we move forward until we know more.”**  He looked at Ciara and then to Ghurn.  **”We could use your help with the *Venture* and with our two friends.   They could be ok or in the hands of Rask and or the Cooperative.
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

“**Up to you,” **said Iyola to Ciara. “**You can listen in during my communication to the Corrigendum, if you want.  Make sure to tell their captain how brave, helpful, and heroic Wes and I were if I call on you, OK?”**  She laughed a little bit at her joke, which wasn’t really that much of a joke.   She wondered whether she should send Ciara and Lis over to the Oya’ka.  Maybe if Wes showed them around.  It might be a bit much having them go over alone, and Misha was going to be busy with the comms.


Iyola listened to Lis, then Wes.  ** “Listen, Lis - can I call you Lis?” ** Iyola said, spreading her hands and placing them on the table, leaning toward him slightly.  **“I get it.  You’re frustrated.  You’re used to knowing things, and now you’re in a time and place where you don’t know what’s going on. **

** "And yes, of course we’re not dealing with Lina Soh’s best out  here in the outer rim, there are smugglers and thieves and syndicates, I’ve worked with them too.  But I’ve got to tell you, there’s a big difference between a petty criminal who overcharges you for coaxium or slips you a fake sylop, and the kind of horrible things Rask has done.  Think torture, slavery, murder.  And believe me, if you are unfortunate enough to meet him, you’ll know that he’s the worst of the worst.  Whatever their reason was for hiring him, it was at best a bad mistake, and that's being generous."**  Her voice sharpened.

**"Oh, and your savior Elara? The whole reason you had to be rescued in the first place is because Elara and Rask showed up on the Wayward Vigil.  Because he’s the one who wrecked it, and all of you would have been dead if Wes, Nadia, and I hadn’t come by!”**

She was getting a bit heated.  She took a deep breath, stood straight, folded her hands, steadied her voice.   “**The goal is to get everyone back safe and sound.  Nadia and Varda back here, and Elara back on her ship doing whatever she does.  Will you help us?”** she asked Lis and Ciara.

*He clicks his tongue, annoyed at the patronising tone.* **"I was in need of rescue the moment I was put in stasis... would you have found me, were it not for Elara?"** *His tongue sharpens alongside the tone, a bit like a dagger.* **"Someone came to me when I was helpless and regardless of the method I am now alive... I trust that this Rask fellow is a scumbag, since he almost killed me... but she saved me, just as you did... I owe her my life just as much as I owe you"** *A sigh softens the tone, he doesn't have the stamina for such things nor the know-how to be tactful, he waves his hand in the air, as if dismissing the conversation.* **"Your soldier, Master Skirata, tells me that this 'Empire' has ships that kill planets or even stars... the galaxy is clearly a much more violent place than it used to be.. I apologize to both you and Captain Kett if my words came out as confrontational... I am merely have yet to get used to... this hopeless and merciless future"**

“**I mean, you would have still been safely asleep, so, no,”  **said Iyola.  “**But I understand.  Honestly, yes, Elara does deserve thanks, because she made your awakening much more bearable. I woke up stark naked, ignorant, and sick to my stomach.” **She bit her lip thoughtfully.  “**Maybe I should bring Elara along to the call with the Corrigendum,” she said.  “What do you all think?  Wes?  Keeping security in mind, of course,”**  she said, looking at Ghurn.  

She visibly startled as Lis casually described the existence of ships capable of destroying planets and stars.  She looked over again at Ghurn, who showed no such surprise.  * So it’s true.  *

 **  “I’ve been awake in this time maybe ten days,”** she said frankly. **“There’s a lot for us Sleepers to learn, and most of it isn’t good.  Ghurn Skirata isn’t *my* soldier, he’s our generous host.  And no need to apologize, Lis. Between a bunch of Mandalorians and me, none of us are particularly sensitive about strong opinions.  I honestly prefer them.” ** She smiled again, this time a bit more easily. 

**“And it’s not hopeless,”** Iyola added.  **“Because I’m here, and I have hope.  I do.” **

Ciara silently observes the back and forth, considering where she might weigh in, if at all. She agrees with Lis, but since the plan is already to get Elara back to her people, there's no need to belabor the point. She lets out a sigh and chooses to address an earlier statement from Iyola, and tie it to the recent topic.

"**I would like to listen in for the call.**" She responds to the request for testimony at face value, despite being aware that it was intended to be lighthearted humor. "**If it's relevant I will vouch that we sleepers and even Elara would be dead if not for your crew.**" Ciara lets a bit of fatigue show on her face, a small sigh escaping her. "**I also think it would be seen as quite the gesture of goodwill if you have Elara present. She can also vouch that you saved her life and haven't treated her poorly.**"

She considers the room and the people again. Mandalorians. She had suspicions before, and now confirmation. She searches her memories, tries to recall how she felt about them. The Mandalorian War was before her time, by the time she was active they were scattered. Mercenaries, mostly. Some of them held a grudge, hated Jedi, felt a need to prove themselves, to recover some lost pride. Some seemed to be just trying to survive, and held a deep respect for Revan and the Jedi that fought along side him. The pride was still there though. 

She sweeps the line of thought away. It has been millennia. They could have nothing in common beyond the name and the general look of the armor. 

She looks to Lis, "**I'm inclined to agree with Iyola. Nothing I have learned has left me bereft of hope."** She mentally adds, *Hope was taken from me before I woke up, with all the rest.* Ignoring the thought, she continues, "**Seems like we're all exceptionally talented people. The Force has brought us together, and I doubt that's an accident. Whatever you wish to hope for, I believe we can accomplish it.**" No hope of her own, no faith. Certainty. It is enough.

At the mention of the Force and things not being an accident, Ghurn’s posture falters for a microsecond. The hardness in his eyes wavers, replaced by a distance that feels heavy with the memory of another Jedi woman who once stood in this compound and spoke of destiny. He clears his throat, the sound a rough grating in the quiet room, and his expression snaps back into a stern, Mandalorian mask. 

**"My sister-in-law used to say the same thing,"** he says, his voice lower than before. **"She believed the Force had a plan. Most days, I think the Force just likes to watch the galaxy bleed. But if you’re both willing to stand with this crew, then the Skiratas will honor that."**

He looks toward the door, where Arno is still visible in the corridor. **"Reluctantly, I agree. If Vance is on the screen, her people might be less inclined to fire first. Arno, go to Room Beta. Bring Elara Vance here."**

Ghurn turns back to the table, tapping a sequence into the master console to open a high-priority channel to the Comm Hub. **"Misha, I’m patching you through to the main display in Briefing Room Gamma. We’re doing this here. Patch the *Corrigendum*’s frequency through the Port Town Array once Vance is in the room."**
Arno offers a sharp, silent nod. The clank of his armored boots recedes as he heads back toward the other briefing room to retrieve Elara.

Wes listened as the group talked.  He nodded slowly as Ciara mentioned the Force and how it brought them together.  His thoughts immediately went to Fakir and Marina.   The Force had brought them together.  Even though the path was short it carved out who they were and what they would become. 


A sadness rose up within him and when Ghurn mentioned his sister-in-law.   Though he didn’t say her name he knew the man talked about Marina.  He felt the sudden and quickly fading sadness from the man and it only served to increase his own sadness.   The sudden burst of emotions broke through his own defensive walls for a moment as sadness and regret quickly poured out and flooded the room.  As Ghurn cleared his throat he took a quick intake of breath as he regained his own composure.  Memories flooded in only to be pushed back down and controlled.    


He continued to stand where he was.  The memory of Marina walking down the gangplank flashed in his mind.  Knowing that she was now gone grated on an exposed nerve.  He looked over at Ciara who probably felt his defenses drop and the emotions spill out only to feel his walls go back up.   His focus came back as Ghurn set the upcoming call into motion.  He waited for it to begin.
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

Ciara observes the emotion sweep through the two soldiers, like a wave, rippling out from her statement about the force. The wave of emotion doesn't reach her, crashing against the invisible wall between her and all emotion, even her own. There had been times in her life that she would have given anything to cut that connection, if only for a little while. She considers that this might be longer than a little while. 

She notices Wes look to her, she recognized that look. People rarely want others intruding on their emotions, and it's always a safe bet the Zeltron is intruding. She gives a sympathetic look, mixed with a bit of guilt. 

The look probably seems fake. She never actually felt guilty about feeling others' emotions, but she understood it often made them feel better if she did. She had never been very good at hiding how she really felt. Then again, right now this guilt was just as real as anything else she tried to convey, maybe it won't look strange. 

She considers imparting some jedi teachings about the nature of the force and why it might give hardships. In her time they felt like platitudes to her. She considers giving her own thoughts. If Elara were already here she would simply say nothing at all, preferring to wait for the call, but there is time, and it seems appropriate to say something. 

She's not even sure if this idea is still around, but voices it anyway "**It is said that when we die, we return to The Force. If that is true, I like to imagine the will of The Force is made up of all those who have returned to it... That those close to me who have died are part of that very force now, keeping an eye on the ones important to them, trying to nudge fate in their favor, ever so slightly.**" She gives a light sigh. "**It might be a strange thing to say about a concept as big and all encompassing as The Force, but, I'm sure it's doing it's best."** She realizes she would never have said that last part around her fellow Jedi. A mistake, perhaps.

Ciara claims that they’re all exceptional people, and that the Force has something to do with them being there.  Iyola thinks about this for a moment.  Jedi are by definition exceptional, and she supposes Mandalorians are too, though not always a good exception.  Lis is apparently a master engineer.  And what is she?  Is she exceptional because now somehow she’s the captain of the Venture?  Was she exceptional before coming here?  *I dropped out of the Survey Corps, I traveled the galaxy with my boyfriend, my parents bought my ship for me.  I thought I was here because the Protectors made a mistake.  If I’m here because of the Force, then the Force is dumb. *

Ghurn growls that his sister in law thought the Force had a plan. Wes doesn’t say anything.  He just looks gloomy.  The tone of the room is getting glum all of a sudden.  It almost makes her sad herself.  She shakes it off. If  she’s not confident, this negotiation won’t go their way. 

Ciara returns to the Force discussion.  Well, she is a Jedi, after all.  She talks of returning to the Force, the Force somehow guiding people - she knows Jedi believe that.  But her?

** “I don’t think the Force wants anything,”** Iyola opined.  **“It’s like water.  It’s everywhere.  Maybe some people can swim in it.  But it just does what it does.” **

She sets her jaw.  “**When Elara comes in, she’ll see that Ciara and Lis are OK, and I can explain what we need, and how they can help.  Misha, if I say a code word, let’s say, Corellia, cut the sound temporarily until I ask you to fix it.  That will let us discuss if we need to.” **

She just hoped Elara would be cooperative.  *No way to know but to try.*

Misha’s voice crackles over the room's speakers, her tone professional and sharp now that she has a technical task to focus on. ***"Understood, Captain. Code word 'Corellia' is locked into the bypass. If triggered, the audio buffer will loop five seconds of ambient static until you give the all-clear. The Port Town Array is slaved to your terminal. I've successfully mirrored the *Aether*'s handshake protocol. To the *Corrigendum*, this will look like an incoming priority burst from Rask's own bridge."***

The door to Briefing Room Gamma hisses open. Arno Skirata enters first, his T-visor scanning the room before he steps aside. Elara Vance follows him. This time, she is not blindfolded. She blinks against the soft light of the room, her eyes darting to Lis and Ciara. A visible wave of relief washes over her. She looks at Iyola, offering a small, appreciative nod for keeping her word before settling into the chair Arno points out at the far end of the table.

Ghurn Skirata steps back from the master console, crossing his arms over his chest. He casts a long, dark shadow over the holographic star chart in the center of the table. He looks at Iyola, his expression as unyielding as iron. **"The line is open. Whenever you're ready, Captain, start the dance."**
The central holographic projector hums, a blue light shimmering in the center of the table, waiting for the connection to bridge the gap between Bespin and the heavy cruiser hidden in the void.

*The engineer eyes are a bit glazed over, though this seems to be a moment of great suspence it kind of all flies over his head... treatises, alliances, tension, drama... he just wish he was back at the ship, dealing with the droids, trying to pry information out of Eve's digital mind.*
*He blinks twice and comes back from his daydreaming as Elara walks in, a good five or so seconds after she has done so, he gives her a nod as she enters, before focusing back on... well, no, before pretending to focus back on the call while instead he thinks back about the intricacies of softwares and hardwares... albeit, a part of him is curious and occasionally shifts him back to the events happening in front of him: maybe something entertaining is about to unfold? Hopefully it won't be like one of the meetings like way back then.*

Iyola nods as Elara enters the room, giving her a small smile.

**“I’m about to get in touch with Captain Sindara,”** she says. **“Stay visible, so they know you’re in good hands.”**

Ciara looks ready to be called upon if needed, while Lis is staring off somewhere else entirely. Ship-to-ship negotiation probably isn’t his thing.   *It’s not hers either, but here they are.*
 
She tilts her head slightly, rolling out the tension in her neck, then focuses on the screen.

**“OK, Misha,” **she says. “**Patch them through.**”

The holographic projector in the center of the table hums, the sound rising from a low thrum to a sharp, electric whine. Particles of blue light dance and collide, weaving together to form a life-sized, flickering image. The surroundings of the caller are absent—only the sharp, authoritative silhouette of a woman is visible, rendered in shades of translucent sapphire.

The woman in the hologram doesn't wait for a visual confirmation on her end. She is leaned forward, her features sharp and her grey-streaked hair pulled back into a severe, functional knot. Her expression is one of controlled, professional fury. **"Rask! If you have one breath of reason left in that thick skull, you will explain immediately why the *Vigil* is currently a cooling cloud of debris and why my crew member has been off-comms for the last four hours."** 

She paces a short, flickering step within the projection's range, her voice like a whip-crack. **"You were hired for extraction, not a demolition. If Elara Vance or her charges have been compromised by your incompetence, there isn't a shadow in the Outer Rim deep enough to hide you from our reach. Speak!"**

The silence in Briefing Room Gamma is absolute as Sindara finishes her tirade. She freezes, her blue-tinged eyes narrowing as the delay in the transmission clears and she realizes she is not looking at the captain of the *Aether*, but a number of strangers—and the very woman she just threatened Rask over.

The hologram flickers as Sindara visibly recoils, her hands going to a console off-screen. Her fury doesn't vanish, but it transforms into a cold, lethal alertness. She looks at Iyola, then at the armored Wes, her gaze finally coming back to Elara. **"Elara? What is this? Who are these people, and why is this signal originating from a mirrored *Aether* relay?"**

Elara stands slowly, her hands resting on the edge of the table so they are visible to the holographic camera. Her voice is calm, acting as the immediate stabilizer for the high-tension communication. **"Sindara. It’s me. I am alive, and I am safe."**

Ghurn remains in the shadows, his arms crossed. He doesn't speak, but his presence is a heavy weight in the room. He looks at Iyola, a silent signal that the floor is now hers. *Don't choke, Captain.*

*A bit of a shit eating grin appears in the engineer's face, as if vindicated by how pissed off the woman in the hologram looks in regards to this ever hated Rask guy.*
*He doesn't say anything though, as bad as he is in social situation he knows full well he shouldn't obstruct such an important call: Iyola is still his captain and any kind of sabotage from his part would be grossly unprofessional, so he wipes off the smile from his face shortly after it surfaces, fixing his posture to be a bit more dignified... of course, he immediately starts to daydream aftewards but at least he's making an attempt.*

Wes suppressed a smile when he heard the sound of the woman’s ire.   He doesn't need the Force to sense the displeasure of Rask in her voice.   That was a good thing and that Ghurn saw it too was fortunate.  With his limited experience with the Mandalorian he had found that Ghurn had an accurate sense of judging people.   


He saw the hitch in her speech as her eyes narrowed.  The realization of who she was looking at or who she wasn’t looking at settled in.  Her fury for Rask focused and Wes saw focus change to who was on the screen.  Elara, Iyola and himself.  He kept quiet after Elara answered the woman.   She called her Sindara and Wes filed that away.   It was now time to play the game and he waited for Iyola to respond.
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

“**Captain Sindara,”** said Iyola, giving the slightest nod.  **“As you can see, none of us are Rask, fortunately.**”  She lightly rested one hand on the conference table, looking steadily at the flickering projection in front of her. 

**“My name is Iyola Kett. Elara and the two people she was sent to find are safe with me.”**  She paused for a brief moment, taking in how this was landing with the captain of the Corrigendum.

**“We’re looking for some people too. I believe we can help each other here.”**
Ciara's face remains passive as the conversation begins. She watches the hologram closely, but doesn't feel the need to give any input.

**"Iyola Kett,"** Sindara says, the name rolling off her tongue like a tally on a ledger. The holographic flickering does little to hide the way her eyes sharpen, cataloging Iyola's casual posture and the defiant set of her jaw. 
She shifts her weight, her silhouette leaning closer to the transmitter. **"We have no love for Rask. He was a tool of necessity, one that proved to be as blunt as it was broken. If you have extracted Elara and the Sleepers from the *Vigil*'s death-throes, you have done what my contractor could not. But let us speak plainly of 'help'."**

The hologram ripples as she gestures to someone off-screen. **"We intercepted a K-Type shuttle. It matches the signature of the craft docked with the *Vigil*. I have the pilot and an elderly woman."** Sindara's expression remains an unreadable mask of translucent blue. **"If Elara is safe, and if those two individuals beside you are the Sleepers we seek, then we have a trade. But I do not negotiate for pieces of a whole, Iyola. I want my scholar and her charges and I want to know where you've hidden the *Venture*."**

Elara had been right-  this Sindara was not one for pleasantries.  

**“First things first,”** said Iyola.**  “I’ve made sure that Elara and the newcomers are here so you can see for yourself that they’re OK.  So show me Nadia and Varda.  I’d like to confirm that they’re all right.” ** She was frankly relieved that Nadia had failed to get herself killed. That was not a sure thing.  **“Then we can continue.” **

Wes stood by Iyola.   He was an imposing figure and he did little to hide that fact.  His stance was loose and his hands rested loosely on his utility belt.    When Sindara mentioned she had the shuttle along with its pilot and the old woman, his eyes narrowed at the image.  He was about to say that the whereabouts of the *Venture* wasn’t her concern but Iyola beat him to it.   Which was for the best.   She didn’t need to know that they had it.  He waited along with everyone else for the woman’s response.
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

Ciara crosses her arms, a flicker of annoyance briefly appearing her face. This captain Sindara is trying to profit from this. She muses that it's good that her emotions are bound. She would be angry, indignant, she would probably speak up right now. 

Her unnatural calm allows her to consider the situation with a level head. She's not a rising star in the most influential group in the galaxy any more. Interrupting the dealings and making a scene can only make things worse right now. 

Perhaps once the deal is concluded she will share her thoughts, but for now, she watches, considering what her options are in a worst case scenario.

The hologram of Sindara flickers with a wave of static as she taps a command on her unseen console. **"Verification is a professional courtesy I am happy to extend, Iyola."** 

The holographic view expands, the resolution sharpening as a second camera feed is patched through. The scene shifts to what appears to be a high-security medical observation ward aboard the *Corrigendum*. Nadia Skirata is standing near a diagnostic bed, her black-and-blue Mandalorian plate looking scuffed but intact. She has her helmet off, her face pale and her expression a jagged mix of shame and suppressed fury. Beside her, Varda Nisyren sits on a low stool, leaning on her staff and looking around the room with an air of distracted, elderly curiosity that Wes and Iyola recognize as a complete fabrication—the seer is cataloging every sensor and guard in the room.
The image snaps back to Sindara’s severe face. **"They are unharmed and under the care of my best medical staff."**

*He raises an eyebrow but didn't seem too surprised... it was weird to have himself be a trade item but at the same time it wasn't that unexpected: why save anyone anyone from stasis if not to make use of their skills? Even the most generous and selfless of organisations requires workers and funds... his gaze lingered towards his current captain and Skirata... maybe he could choose his boss?*
*His head tilted left and right for a few seconds... but ultimately it was Iyola's crew that saved him even if Elara's organisation started his rescue operation... he shooed these thoughts from his mind: already thinking and potential work politics was too tedious for the engineer, better stick to this and call it a day.*
*However, he did focus more on Elara, to see her reaction, he was curious at how she was reacting to this situation.*

**“Thank you, Sindara,**” said Iyola.  “**They certainly look like they’re enjoying your hospitality.” ** Seeing Nadia alive would only mollify Ghurn for so long.  *I need to make sure Nadia and Varda are freed.*

**“So this is how I see it,**” says Iyola.  **“Elara is with us, and she wants to return to you.  Nadia and Varda are with you, and they want to return to us.”**

She looks over at Lis and Ciara.  Ciara is clearly invested in the conversation.  She’s sitting defensively, with her arms crossed; if Iyola had to guess, she didn’t enjoy that bit about being a “charge” either.  Lis was looking over at Elara.  Maybe he was still attached to her, but that was his choice. 

**“As for these two,”** she said, indicating Ciara and Lis with one efficient motion, **“they aren’t on the board.  You wanted them rescued, well, they’re rescued!  And now they can decide what they want to do and where they want to go.”**

**“Let’s keep it simple.  We make sure everyone is where they want to be. Nadia and Varda come back on their shuttle, you get Elara back,  the new people decide what they want to do.  And there you go, a successful rescue mission.”**

The blue light of the hologram flickers, casting a rhythmic strobe across Sindara’s sharp features. She lets out a slow, measured breath, the fury from moments ago cooling into a hard, professional pragmatism. 

**"A 'successful rescue mission.' You have a colorful way of describing a series of catastrophic failures and mutual abductions, Iyola Kett,"** Sindara says, her voice regaining its smooth, persuasive cadence. She looks at Lis and Ciara, her gaze lingering on them for a long, silent heartbeat. **"The Cooperative is not the Protector Order. Our mandate is built on the principle that the 'Mantle of Self' is inviolable. They have their choice."**

She shifts, leaning closer to the terminal on her end. **"I accept your terms. Elara Vance for the pilot and the elder. But let us be honest: you are currently floating in a stolen ship without teeth, and Rask did not leave the Dreva Sector empty-handed. He has the *Vigil* Archive Core—a map of the entire Sleeper web. If you want to know why you were taken, or where the rest of your history is hidden, you need that core as much as we do."**

The hologram star chart shifts to show a remote coordinate. **"There is a decommissioned relay station in a nearby nebula. It is off the Imperial charts and neutral to our interests. Bring Elara there. We will exchange personnel and discuss a more... permanent arrangement regarding Captain Rask."**

Elara stands up, looking at the flickering image of her commander with a mix of loyalty and wariness. She looks back at Iyola and Wes. **"She's right about the core. Rask is an opportunist, but he's smart enough to know that map is worth more than the Sleepers themselves."**

Wes looked at the image of Nadia that was on the screen.  She looked alright and unharmed.  If anything she seemed more upset with herself for getting caught and not reaching Rask.   Varda on the other hand brought the slightest of smiles to his face.  She was playing the addled old woman to a tee.  If they knew the strength of the old witch, not to mention Nadia as well, they would be holding both of the women under a very heavy watch.  


He continued to listen as Iyola and Sindara came to a resolution about Elara and the other two.  Sindara's assertion about a stolen ship with no teeth spoke of some truth.  The weapon systems on the *Venture* were non-functioning.  He also doubted that Ghurn, despite his resources and contacts, could get the ship and Eve into fighting shape. 


He looked at Iyola and back at the screen after Elara spoke about Rask.  **”Rask will get what’s coming for him.”**  The smooth tone of his voice sounded out.  **”Whether it's by you, us or both of us only time will tell.”**
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

Ciara exhales a long sigh, letting out a breath she didn't know she had been holding. Things seemed to work out remarkably well. Suspiciously well, honestly. Sindara seemed like she was playing for keeps then immediately relented on all the extra things she wanted, leaving Ciara skeptical, but the deal is made for now. 

She will probably be joining for this mission, and can try to prevent any potential betrayal. She never liked talking to people through the holo, made it harder to get a read on what they were really feeling. 

She opts to continue her silence for now, planning to only interact during the call if someone directly addresses her, or voices a question in her area of expertise, which seems very unlikely.

***"I wouldn't say without teeth... I'd be surprised if that ship doesn't have a trick up its sleeve..."*** *As much as the engineer was trying to be silent, he can't help but whisper to himself as he muses the capabilities of both the ship and Eve... maybe it was a stretch to think it hid some high powered blaster but the AI had hidden an entire section of the ship from the previous engineer... after all warfare isn't just ballistics and with all the heuristic processors used, Garah wondered if Eve couldn't perform some hacking or some other form of nightmarish data attack.*
*He grits his teeth, he wants to share his thoughts pretty bad but it being not the right time or the right place ties his hands with endless frustration.*

“**Mantle of Self,”**  Iyola said slowly, rolling the pretentious phrase around in her mouth.**  “Not totally sure what that means, but sounds good.”  
**
As Sindara transmitted the coordinates, she eyed them, an idea coming to mind.  **“That  relay looks familiar - near the run to Corellia?”  **

She waited a microsecond to see if Misha’s code works.  If it does, she immediately said over the comm to Misha, **“Check those coordinates - make sure we can approach safely, map the place out. Ghurn, if you know anything about that station, now’s the time to share.”   **

After the signal clears, Iyola notes ** “Signal hiccup.  Back to our conversation.” ** She shrugs.  **“Assume what you want about the Venture, but I’ll tell you one thing, it’s ours.** “ She smiled a bit, nothing big, just enough to take the edge off.   “**Remember to have Nadia’s shuttle ready for us too - if you think she’s grouchy now, try taking her vessel.”**

“ **Oh and - “** she raised one finger.  **“One more thing - if Rask was working for you, why’d he take the Core for himself?”**

She let her hand fall, relaxed, by her side.  **“ See you at the station, Sindara.”**

It’s a labyrinth of docking arms and cold corridors."**
***"Privacy window closing in five... four... three... reconnecting."***

The subtle hiccup in the signal passes, and the flickering image of Captain Sindara stabilizes. She appears unmoved by the brief interference, her focus remaining on the deal.**"Rask is a scavenger who realized he was holding more than just a paycheck,"** Sindara answers, her voice hardening with a cold, sharp edge. **"The *Vigil* Archive Core is a map of every hidden cache, every Sleeper's location, and every encryption key used by the Protectors in this sector. She straightens her posture, the translucent blue light of the hologram pulsing as she prepares to terminate the link. **"He is heading for a black-market hub to move the asset. If you truly wish to see these 'Forgotten Ones' free, Iyola Kett, you will see the value in ensuring Rask doesn't hand that map to the Empire."** 

The hologram begins to dissolve into particles of light. **"Two hours. Do not be late. *Corrigendum* out."**

As Sindara’s hologram flickers to nothingness, Iyola allows her body to relax fractionally.   That could have gone a lot worse.  But it’s not like she can kick back with *The Green Veil *now.

**“I wonder what trade hub she was talking about,”**  Iyola mused aloud.  **“I bet Nadia would know.  She was on his trail.” **  She turned to the people around her.

**“Suggestions?    Elara, do you know anything about this station meeting point?  Ghurn, Wes, your  thoughts on what we should do here?” **

She looked over at Lis and Ciara.  **“Lis, it looks like we’ll have a few hours.  I want to show you something Misha and I have been working on.  I have a feeling you’ll like it.”  **

**“Ciara, I know it’s not easy to get a read on someone you’ve only met through a holo,  but what’s your take on Sindara?”  ** She pauses for a moment, remembering that she’s not supposed to talk about Jedi explicitly.  **“ I imagine you’re better at that in person.”  **

There’s one thing that bothers her. ** “Something I don’t get.  Why would Rask go to the Empire?  Isn’t he a criminal?  Wouldn’t they just arrest him?”**

*As he was still thinking a few things though, he seems to reawaken from his daydreaming.* **"Mhn? Ah? Oh! That should be interesting... as for Rask, I believe he likely want to trade whatever information he has to have his crime history erased and become a rich and respectable citizen..."** *He shrugs.* **"Apparently Jedis nowadays are very hated so selling a map to every last one remaining could net some credit or two... or maybe he thinks the Empire could use various experts from different places in time... if even a tenth of the sleepers had my level of career in different fields... well, wouldn't that be a great technological leap forward?"**
*He taps his chin a couple of times.* **"So... just to clarify... if we meet this Rask in the future... what should be the modus operandi? Do we shoot him in the head? Capture for interrogation? I think this is a good time to clarify what everyone wants from the man... I'd honestly be fine just..."** *He turns his hands into finger guns.* **"Pew pew... you know? But a man like that is good at manipulation, so he probably will say something about having hidden the map or some other crap... so yeah, let's be on board on how to handle him in a situation were we can't communicate"**

Ciara considers the end of the conversation, then Iyola's question to her. Something close to amusement creeps onto her face as Iyola adds the last part directed at her. "**Yes, we Zeltron unfortunately can't read emotions through the holo, so in person is much better.**" 

She glances at Elara, wondering how attached she is to Sindara. Ultimately, she decides that's irrelevant right now. A strong exhale from her nose accompanies her attention returning to Iyola. "**I won't know for sure until I meet her in person, so take it with a grain of salt. This might just be habit from my own time, but I don't trust her. She seemed very hard line about what she was willing to accept, she presented herself as unwilling to compromise on that. Then she immediately caved and accepted your first counter offer. I think it's likely she still has a play in mind to get the ship, if nothing else. I would go in expecting complications, even if it's not explicitly a trap.**" 

Her mind churns out possibilities. "**A tracker on the prisoners or some other item so she can find the ship after we separate, it could also be attached to the vessel we use for the rendezvous while we're dealing with the exchange, that's the most obvious. It's probably unlikely, but she could have someone waiting to collect the ship while we're away doing this trade... I could go on, but I think you get the picture.**" 

She has no input on how the Empire operates relative to criminals, so she can't even begin to speculate about the Rask situation. "**Surely the Empire deals with some criminals. Even if Rask isn't one of those, perhaps he knows of one who can act as a middleman.**"

Wes listened as the others talked about Rask and whether he would go to the Empire despite being a criminal.  **”Why wouldn’t he?  Governments will use whatever resources they can to keep the status quo.   Doesn’t matter if they are above or below the table.”**  He looked at Iyola.   **”The Empire will want to control anything that will give them the ability to keep their grip on their power.  While the Cooperative seems like they give sleepers the option to choose their path the Empire won’t.   They will bend and push the sleepers to work for them.  If they don’t they would have no use.”**


He looked at Lis.  The engineers question what to do with Rask if and when they come across the pirate.  **”We take him alive if at all possible.”**  He looked over at Ghurn.  **”The pirate has information and is a significant cog in a wheel that could provide information.  Ghurn, I have a question.”**. He looked at Iyola and then back to the Mandalorian.  **”We may need more fighters when we go.  If Iyola is okay with it I’d like to request a squad of your warriors.   If an opposing party knows they will potentially have to face off with Mandalorians it will give them pause to push violence.”**
-# <:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405><:spacer:1312665322142306405> <:fett:1287130263851368489> Off

And you’re right, Rask would have a fair amount of useful information. I also have to wonder how she knew anything about our ship’s status. Either they scanned us, or they’ve been tracking us longer than we think.”  **

Ciara and Wes opined that the Empire was open to working with criminals.  Iyola sighed. “**This isn’t how a government should behave. Cue Ciara’s question.

**“I don’t know what we’ll do with the core,” **Iyola admitted. **“But I know what we won’t do. We don’t sell it. We don’t exploit it. I hope we can use it to get answers. And help others like us.”**

**"Grist is a blunt instrument, Captain,"** Ghurn rumbles, his dark eyes momentarily shifting to the security feed of the holding cells. **"He didn't know much, but he confirmed one thing: the ambush on Nadia and Derek wasn't a coincidence. Elara looks between Iyola and Ghurn, her expression strained. **"I have never been to Station 7-Delta. Please... do not mistake her pragmatism for malice."**

**"We'll see soon enough,"** Ghurn says, turning to the master console. **"Misha, you heard the Captain. We jump in thirty minutes. *He nods with a faint, pleased smile.* **"Very well... worst case scenario, if the batteries are bust just give me a couple million potatoes... should work..."** *He chuckles to himself sofly, not waiting for anyone else to be amused: the joke was more for him than the others.* **"Where are the capacitors then? **“Thank you, Ghurn,” ** Iyola said sincerely.  It was a relief knowing that the Atin Oyaka would be backing them up.  Of course, she knew his interest was because of Nadia, but she was still grateful. 

She turned to Elara**. “No malice on this side toward Sindara and your ship either, Elara,**” she said.  “**I am not sure I can say the same for the beings she hires.” ** She paused for a moment.  **“So she’s focused on Lis and Ciara? **“So 30 minutes to jump, 2 hours to arrival?**” she clarified. ** “I guess it’s back to the Venture.”**

Ciara holds her further comments about Sindara, without meeting the woman in person she can't know for sure. Perhaps her era has left her more mistrustful of others than she should be... or perhaps her immediate company are naïve. Honestly, she hopes for the former. 

At Iyola's last statement, Ciara adds a nod. "**If there is something even more unique about us compared to the other sleepers, even if only in their perception of us, I'd like to know it as well.**"

**"Precisely,"** Ghurn confirms, checking a data-chit before handing it to Arno. **"Thirty minutes until departure. Two hours in lightspeed."** He looks to Wes, a heavy hand clapping onto the Mandalorian's shoulder. **"Arno is under your command for the duration of this flight. Keep a tight perimeter. I’m heading to the *Oya'ka* now."** With a final, sharp nod to Iyola, the Skirata patriarch strides out of the room.

Elara turns to Ciara, her expression earnest. **"It isn't that you are necessarily 'more' unique than Iyola or Wes, Ciara. It’s that the Cooperative has limited reach. We have been tracking the *Wayward Vigil*’s specific ionic signature for several months. To Sindara, you are the culmination of a very long, very difficult search. She sees it as her duty to ensure you have the resources to survive this era."**

Wes watched as Ghurn passed a data chit to Arno.  The Mandalorian wondered what it contained.  While he did consider Ghurn an ally he knew the man would do whatever it took to make sure Nadia was recovered.   Those who weren’t Mandalorian didn’t always understand how important it was to them.    


A second later Wes felt the heavy hand of the Skirata patriarch impact his left pauldron.  The one that was emblazoned with his own clans sigil.  Ghurn’s words were short and to the point.  Wes would have command of Arno and the squad that were coming with him.  **”Understood.  I hope we won’t need their expertise.  If we do, we’ll make them pay.”**


As Ghurn moved to leave Elara started to speak to Ciara.  Picking up his helmet he turned to Arno and moved closer, holding out his hand and forearm to the Mandalorian.  **”Grab your gear and round up the rest of your team.  There could potentially be Force users there.  Equip for that possibility as well.  I’ll see you on the Venture.”**  He watched as Arno gave him a sharp nod and quickly turned to go.   


With practiced ease he put his helmet on, securing it into place.  As he moved closer to Iyola and the others as they spoke.

She turns her focus back to Iyola. **"You want to reach her, Captain? Show her you aren't the Protectors. If you want her cooperation, you don't need to 'charm' her. You simply need to show her that you have no intention of delivering the *Venture* or the data cores back into the hands of the Protectors. If she believes you are truly independent of their design, she will release Nadia and Varda without hesitation."**


She looks at Ciara in the corner, her hazel eyes holding a quiet, maternal understanding. **"A healthy caution is what kept you alive during your war, Ciara, but do not let it become a wall that keeps you from seeing friends.**

Misha lets out a short, surprised laugh at the mention of an "engineer pyramid scheme," her eyes crinkling as some of her persistent tension finally dissolves. **"I'm pretty sure Ghurn Skirata would have a heart attack if we tried to put 'Dyson Sphere' on the expense report, Lis, but I’d be happy to help you once we have our ship back in one piece."** 

She shakes her head as they approach the recreation room, her fingers hovering over the door control as she addresses his question about titles. Her face flushes slightly. **"I'm just 'Misha,' Lis. I don't have a title. I'm not a 'Master.' I'm just a junior technician. I dropped out of the Imperial Academy on Coruscant before I could finish my advanced certifications. Calling you 'Master Garah' is... well, it's out of respect."** 

She taps the interface, her voice dropping into a quieter, professional tone as the heavy door hisses open. **"The simulation pods are along the back wall. If the Captain and Wes are in here, we’ll need to let them know we’re shutting down the pod’s grid to harvest the stabilizers."**

Ciara says that she’s not interested in playing dejarik, as she has a lot of reading to do.  It makes sense.  She has thousands of years to catch up on.  Iyola still can’t believe it herself - a Jedi who served with Revan.  That said, the variants Iyola had proposed didn’t work well with three people.  **“Some other time,”** Iyola says. “**And I’d like to hear more about the Jedi from your era.   Good news is, there’s plenty of wariness and strife to go around in this new time, apparently, so you’ll have lots of company.” **

Speaking of wariness - Elara gives her some real information about Sindara. **“Historians and researchers? Then we’re aligned, Elara,**” says Iyola. ** “Do you think I’m some kind of merc or soldier?  I’m from the SurveyCorps, for kriff’s sake.”** *Or I was, anyway. * “**And the Protectors are the last beings in the galaxy I’m giving the Venture to.   I don’t know how to prove a negative, but we’ve got info we’re willing to share too.” ** Like that weird bio-chained core she found on the Wayward Vigil.  She’s keeping the nanites, though. They’re too useful, and too much fun.

Wes is looming in full armor, helmet on, making a visual point about the newly martial times. “**One more thing.  As you can see,”** she lightly knocked on Wes’s pauldron to illustrate, “**there are a bunch of Mandalorians here and their diplomatic vocabulary isn’t as, ah, nuanced, as mine. Fortunately, they apparently report to the captain for the time being. Wes, let’s go see the squadron and tell them they have to settle for getting Nadia back without violence.”**
The Corrigendum

The transition from hunter to hunted was as swift as a shadow crossing the Bespin sun. 

When Varda Nisyren (<@807720782364540951>) Nisyren stepped aboard the *Dha’kad* beside Nadia, the young Mandalorian's mind was a cold, sharp blade of focus, her teeth set on a single, desperate task: tracing Captain Rask’s retreating wake before his scent vanished into the void of the Dreva Sector. The blocky, armored shuttle had dropped away from the *Venture*’s hull with a silent, heavy grace, plunging into the thick violet fogs of the ship graveyard like a dark arrow. 

But Rask's scent was not the only thing waiting in the mists. 

As the shuttle prepared to align for its micro-jump, a massive, charcoal-grey shadow materialized—*The Corrigendum*. A brilliant beam of ionized azure energy had erupted from the cruiser’s bow, but Nadia’s response was a masterpiece of reflex. The *Dha’kad* executed a violent, kinetic snap-roll that defied its brutal aerodynamics, the tractor beam missing their engines by mere meters as Nadia punched them into the safety of hyperspace. 

The escape, however, was temporary. The massive cruiser did not lose the trail; it folded space on a parallel vector, shadowing the shuttle's ion wake with terrifying precision. When the *Dha'kad* finally dropped back into realspace, its engines straining and its hyperdrive spent, *The Corrigendum* was already waiting. The second trap snapped shut instantly. The blue-white light of the tractor beam locked onto the shuttle, dragging them into the cruiser's ventral hangar bay. The hunt was over; Rask had vanished into the dark, and they were captured.

Instead of a grimy brig, Varda and Nadia were escorted through quiet, white-lit corridors by silent, professional guards whose grey armor bore the sign of the Interlocking Flame. They were placed in an observation suite—a high-tech, spacious annex that smelled faintly of clean plasteel and active medical scanners. Varda's gnarled staff of Atollon wood, with its dull grey beskar rings, was allowed to remain with her, the guards dismissively assuming the ancient branch was merely a necessary crutch for a centenarian. Nadia, meanwhile was stripped of her visible weaponry and her hands mannacled in front.

For the last hour, the room has been a study in contrasted energy. Nadia has not sat once. She paces the floor with the jerky, erratic rhythm of a caged tooka, her gloved fingers tracing the seam of her gauntlet, her jaw set so tight it vibrates against her teeth. In the Force, her presence is a jagged, freezing needle of shame and suppressed fury, her defensive walls highly visible to Varda's second sight. 

By contrast, Varda remains on her low stool, her hands clasped over the head of her staff, playing the part of the dotty, disoriented grandmother. But beneath her closed lids, her Senses have been tracing the ship's heartbeat. Through the floor plates, she can feel the rhythmic, low-frequency hum of a massive reactor, and closer—just beyond the adjacent bulkhead—the strange, cold hollow of a high-end medical wing that makes her ache with a peculiar, familiar coldness.

The door remains sealed, the room silent. They are alone for now, left with their thoughts and the hum of the ship’s engines as it travels toward its unknown destination.

As Iyola stressed that the *Venture* wasn’t being handed off to anyone, Wes nodded.  The impartial T visor of his helmet made a slight movement up and down.  The fact that Sindara still needed allies was a point in their favor.  One that they could use.   Recovering Nadia and Vadra was the top priority.   Anything else was simply a bonus.  


As Iyola knocked on his pauldron to make a point he turned his head and looked at her.  She was making a point that she wasn’t a blood thirsty grunt or mercenary.   He just wished she wasn’t doing it at his or his fellow Mandalorians' expense.   It was something he would bring up with her at a later time.   

**”You will also play a part in this.  You’ve seen that we aren’t with the Protectors with your own eyes.  We all know that Sindara will value what you have to say in this matter.”**  His helmet's vocoder made his voice sound cold and unemotional.  He turned to Iyola.  **”Let's go find Arno and the others.  Let them know what we are expecting.”** 


As they left the rec room Wes reached up and removed his helmet.  Several steps later he looked at Iyola.  **”I wanted to mention something about Arno and his squad.   I know Ghurn put them under our command and guidance but we’re talking about Nadia here.   His family.  Their family.  Don’t be surprised if he gave them orders to get her back at any cost.  She is family to him and family is a special thing to us.  It’s one of the most valuable things we have.”**  He paused for a second before adding.  **”And she is not just their family.  I know we have a mission to do and that has priority.  I just wanted you to be aware of what I was thinking about Ghurn and that possibility.”**

*He waves his hand dismissively* **"Practice is worth more than grammar anyhow... and regardless, whatever recognition this Empire has is... well, no offense but I heard they are working on *Starkillers*, which apparently are meant to destroy either stars or planet, any governing body willing to spend such resources for the purpose of destroying MORE resources hardly warrant respect..."** *He rubs his chin for a second, as he follow Misha ahead, letting her lead the way.* **"Tell you what... I did teach apprentices and signed off many an internship... I doubt anything with my signature has any value beyond symbolic, but if you want to earn a title I could mentor you! Usually the works for an apprentice is... a bit less than two thousand hours I believe?"** *He taps his chin* **"However special regards were taken for those that were part of an active crew, as a way to legitimize those that started to work before they got a formal education... that should be about two months worth of work since the internship would be considered ongoing even during rest as you'd still be on-duty... so yeah, two months! Then I'll write you a piece of paper giving you a full title as Engineer based on Republic standards!"** *Though it would be far too easy to mistake his attitude for a patronizing one, the glimmering in his eyes and the pride in his voice signal less a dominant attitude and rather a willingness to spread his old tradition, even as he recognize them obsolete in the face of this cruel future.*
**"Want me to inform them?"** *He says, his tone losing a bit of luster yet remaining fairly confident.*

Varda closes her eyes as she leans back. **"Ahh, now these are the times, Nadia. Some of your closest friends can be made when being taken prisoner. Mmhm. In fact this reminds me of that one time back in the old days, you know…"**

She waves one ancient hand vaguely through the sterile air of the suite trying to pluck the memory from somewhere above her head. **"Oh what was the moon called… dreadful little place. Smelled of engine grease and sour Correlion wine. Or perhaps that was Araden."**
A faint smile curls at the corners of her mouth. **"Yes. Yes, that sounds more right."**

Her cloudy eyes remain closed as she continues, her voice drifting into the slow, winding cadence of a grandmother settling into a favourite tale. **"This was before his knees began clicking whenever he stood up, mind you. He still insisted he was young then. Thought because he could still wrestle a cargo crate into place without throwing his back out that the galaxy owed him another century of heroics. Ahh well we were both younger then I suppose. Bah I could still walk without this daft thing. Could still command fear into those who deserve it. Aging is dreadful dear, I do not recommend it."**

She gives a dry little chuckle to herself. **"And Lyra… ah, stars preserve me, Lyra could not have been older than twenty-five. Newly appointed senator of her world. Full of ideals. Full of speeches. With her, diplomacy could solve anything if she simply frowned at people hard enough."**

Varda tilts her head slightly toward Nadia though her expression stays pleasantly distant.**"She had inherited her father’s stubbornness unfortunately. Terrible condition in both bloodlines."**

The hum of the ship fills the small pause that follows. **"We had only meant to stop for supplies. Tibanna gas filters, if I recall. Or perhaps tea. One forgets these things after enough decades."** She taps one finger absently against the head of her staff. **"Anyway. We docked at this little refueling station hanging over the edge of nowhere. Mind you we were meant to be celebrating a successful year. And you know what the spirits decided to throw at us. We found the whole place in chaos. Alarms blaring. Dockworkers screaming. These ruddy pirates, four of them if I recall, all accusing one another of betrayal while pretending not to smuggle spice through the lower bays."**

Her lips purse thoughtfully. **"Poorly pretending, I should add. If an old woman could tell, then frankly they deserved to be arrested."**

A long sigh escapes her. **"Unfortunately one of the pirate captains mistook Araden for a weapons broker he was expecting."**

Her eyes crack open just slightly now, gleaming with faint amusement beneath heavy lids. **"Oh he hated that. Spent twenty minutes insisting he was a respectable merchant while wearing full beskar and glaring at everyone like he wanted to snap necks."**

She shakes her head fondly. **"Made it very difficult to defend his innocence."**

Nadia’s pacing continues, but Varda does not look at her directly. The old witch simply keeps speaking into the room as though the story itself is the important thing. **"Well naturally the pirates decided we were involved. So they kidnapped Lyra, which… Naturally she found deeply offensive as a sitting senator. So they went and shoved the three of us aboard this miserable freighter full of stolen medical supplies and caged nexu."**

A beat passes. **"Yes. Nexu. Puppies to be exact"** Her face wrinkles.

**"Horrid smell. Very furry creatures."** She shifts slightly on the stool, settling deeper into the performance of frailty even as the Force quietly stretches through the walls around her like invisible roots. Feeling. Listening. Counting footsteps beyond the corridor.

**"Now Lyra was furious, of course. She spent the first hour cursing like the best of them while Araden quietly planned which airlock to throw people out of."** Another tiny smile.

**"And me?"** Varda folds her hands atop her staff. **"I spoke to the Nexu."**

The statement lands with complete seriousness. **"Well what else was I to do? Everyone was shouting. One of the pirates had been stabbed. The nexu kept screaming. It was all terribly stressful."** She lets out a small hum.

**"So I sat down and talked to the little rascals."** Her smile sharpens just a fraction beneath the warmth.

**"It turns out most beings become remarkably cooperative when the cages suddenly unlock through some great design from the force. Then said creatures in the cages all line up like dogs trained from birth. infront of a frail old woman"*** Now her gaze slides toward Nadia fully for the first time.

**"Would you like to talk now?"** Varda asks softly. The old woman shifts slightly on her stool, the ancient wood of her staff creaking beneath her weathered hands.

Her expression softens once more. **"if you tell me you want Rask dead, I will not clutch pearls and speak to you of serenity. If you wish to see his bones salted and his spirit cast screaming into a storm of fire…"**

For just an instant something ancient and green flickers deep behind her eyes. **"…then perhaps we can make it so."**

The air grows colder for half a heartbeat before easing again. Varda sighs and leans back slightly, once more appearing every bit the tired old grandmother aboard a ship she has no business being on. **"But sometimes that drive is a fire, Nadia. Useful for warmth. Useful for war.”** Her gaze settles gently on her again. **”Just be certain it is not the thing steering your hands. Fires care very little what they consume."**

Misha's face flushes a deep, warm crimson under the grime on her cheeks as Lis Garah (<@273935660920995841>) offers her a formal apprenticeship under the old Republic standards. To a girl who felt like a discarded, flagged cog in the Imperial system, his words are a lifeline of profound validation. **"An... an apprentice?"** she stammers, her fingers trembling slightly on her datapad. **"Lis, I... I would be honored. I don't care if the Empire doesn't recognize the signature. I will."** She offers him a shy, incredibly genuine smile before the recreation room doors slide open, cutting off her blush.

The heavy door to the Recreation Room irises open with a clean, pneumatic sigh, and the soft, warm light of the room spills into the corridor. Misha and Lis step through the threshold just as Wes  is tucking his helmet under his arm, concluding his quiet, serious warning to Iyola about the Skirata squad’s true loyalties. 

The convergence is immediate. The sterile, quiet sanctuary is suddenly filled with the technical energy of the lower decks.

Misha steps forward, her gaze immediately locking onto Captain Iyola. **"Captain, Wes... we have a solution for the point-defense turrets. Master Garah ran a full diagnostic on the central bus and mapped out a constant-load bypass."** She points toward the back wall where the dark, silent simulation pods are recessed. **"But the plan requires us to harvest the high-throughput stabilizers from one of these pods. It will disable the simulation systems permanently, but it will give us our guns back. We need your authorization to begin the extraction."**

The heavy, rhythmic pacing of Nadia’s boots on the smooth deck plating comes to a sudden, jarring halt. She stands in the center of the room, her back to Varda, her shoulders rising and falling with a slow, trembling breath. Her hands, still locked in the binders, clench into fists so tight the leather groans. 

For a long moment, the only sound is the low, indifferent thrum of the *Corrigendum*’s hyperdrive. Nadia cannot ignore the shift in the room's energy. She felt the ancient, green fire that flickered behind Varda’s eyes—the raw, cold weight of a power that predates the modern galaxy. It doesn't frighten her; instead, it acts like a lightning rod, drawing the chaotic, white-hot static of her own rage down into the durasteel of the floor.

Slowly, she turns to face Varda. Her pale face is cast in the soft blue light of the observation monitors, her blue eyes bright with unshed tears. The mask of the disciplined Mandalorian soldier has finally slipped, revealing the raw, bleeding trauma beneath.

**"I want him to burn,"** Nadia whispers. Her voice is low, cracked, stripped of all armor. **"I want to feel the metal of his ship melt under my hands. I want him to know what Derek felt when the current took his breath. I want to see the light go out in his eyes, and I want to be the one who did it."**

She moves toward the diagnostic bed, her knees suddenly looking weak as she practically drops onto the edge of the sterile mattress. She looks down at her bound hands, her shoulders slumping. 

**"But... there is a static in my head, Varda. It’s like my brain is full of broken glass."** She looks up at the old woman, her gaze desperate, searching for the wisdom Varda’s story promised. **"Every time I try to think about *why* we were there, the noise gets louder. It hurts to even try. How do I steer the fire, Varda, when I don't even know who is holding the matches?"**

*He clears his throat, after Misha.* **"Of course this modification SHOULD be something we can reverse afterwards with the appropriate spare parts... however, I would be failing in my role if I didn't mention there is a degree of risk in this modification, since it would bypass several safety measures and while it is improbable that will result in any immediate emergency if we were to suffer a series of quite specific unfortunate events then it could be quite the problem..."** *He checks his commpad, for a quick review, to make sure he didn't forget anything.* **"...very improbable... albeit it will definitively void any warranty the ship may have..."**

Wes takes off his helmet, thankfully.  It’s nice to see his face.  Iyola is so over all this helmet shit.  It makes Wes sound like a mech droid. 

He warns her that the Mandalorian squad is likely to prioritize the rescue of Nadia over everything else.  **“I know, Wes,” **she says gently. **“Do you think I don’t know that?  That’s why we need to talk to them.  Nadia is a Skirata and family takes priority for Mandalorians.  Your family too, right? Isn’t Nadia part of yours?”**  She paused. “**I don’t want violence to be a first resort. I don’t want the Mandalorian squadron to split off and go rogue.  We need to get that message across to Arno, Kella, and the rest.”**

She turns towards Wes. His dark eyes are sharp, a bit tired, and he’s young, maybe younger than her. It strikes her how odd it is, how much she looks to this Mandalorian man that she’s known for two weeks, give or take.  His support is vital - she’s pretty sure Ghurn would not be humoring her without Wes’s backing - but she still doesn’t know what he thinks of her, if anything.  

It’s at that point that Lis and Misha emerge.  They look delighted.  It appears that mucking around weapons systems is restorative for mechanics.  Misha and Lis report that they can make repairs to the weapons systems - if they disassemble one of the recreation pods.  She hasn’t even had the chance to try out those pods.  Still, she knows what the right thing to do is.  She nods.

**“Amazing.  Yes, proceed,” **she says.  **“Great work.” ** She isn’t sure what a warranty is, but assumes it’s not important.  “**Go void that warranty. Wes and I are going to coordinate with the Mandalorian squadron for a few.”  
**

Wes looked at Iyola as she spoke.  Her first words were spoken softly.  He wasn’t sure if it was because of the added crew and what they were talking about or if spit was a bit of weariness settling in.   The Force knew that they had been going hard since being brought out of cryo but he could see in her eyes she was thinking along the same lines he was.  


Her question about Nadia being family causes him to look up for a second before looking back down.   He could see her eyes assessing him.  Of the group that woke up on the *Venture* that were still around.  They were the closest in age if not in timeline.  Seeing how she took to being captain he knew he made a good choice to support that.  **”It’s complicated.”**  He was about to expand on that subject a little more but they were interrupted by Misha and Lis.


What followed was the energetic explanation from Misha and then a more subdued rundown from Lis.   Apparently the two were making a good team when it can to fixing things.  The added benefit of some defensive weaponry pits them in a better situation overall.   As Iyola gave them the go ahead he found himself also nodding in agreement.  **”Give us an update when you have them online.  The plan is that we won’t need any weapons but having them in case we do puts us in a better situation.”**

Varda stands and leans her staff carefully against the stool beside her. The old wood clacks softly against the metal floor before settling still.

Slowly, with the measured care of age, she lowers herself beside Nadia on the narrow diagnostic bed. One weathered hand moves over Nadia’s bound fists, her touch warm despite the cold sterility of the room. Varda takes a long, deep breath before speaking.**"Amongst being a mother, a grandmother, and a Dathomirian… I am also a healer."**

Her voice is soft now. Simply honest. **"The brain is a mighty organ. One that eclipses nearly all wonders in this world."**

She pauses, her pale eyes studying Nadia gently. **"But at its core… our most basic instinct is to live."** The final word lands with quiet certainty. Ancient and immovable as stone.

**"Some wounds cut so deeply… become so tangled within the soul… that the mind decides remembering them is more dangerous than losing them."** Her thumb brushes once against Nadia’s knuckles. **"And so it hides them away from us."** The hum of the cruiser fills the silence between them.

Varda murmurs. **"The mind does not always preserve what happened. Sometimes it preserves what allows us to continue. Even if it leaves us feeling like we are stranded."**

At last she releases Nadia’s hands and gently wraps an arm around her shoulders, drawing the younger woman against her side with slow grandmotherly tenderness. **"You may remember one day,"** she says quietly. **"There are techniques. Rituals. Medicines. Paths through the fog. We could try to pierce it together if that is what you wish."**

Her gaze drifts toward the sterile white wall ahead, though her focus seems far beyond it. **"Or perhaps the memories will never fully return. Perhaps the glass will continue to rattle for a long while yet."** A faint sigh escapes her.

**"But glass, like all things, is worn smooth by time. Even mountains surrender eventually to wind and water."** Her arm tightens slightly around Nadia. **"You will live, Nadia. You will hurt, and heal, and grow old, and become someone new from this pain. That is the nature of life."** Her pale eyes lower, thoughtful and distant.

**"The Force itself does no less. It rises. It falls. It blooms into being and returns again to the great current from which all things come."** Then, quieter still **"We are not meant to remain unchanged by suffering, my dear. Only to survive it without letting it devour what we are. You say you do not know who is holding the matches. Yet the only one who can is you. I care not if it is the mandorian, the jedi, or the avenging friend. They are all a part for you. You will change but no matter how much you change. You will always be yourself."**

For a long, silent moment, the only movement in the room is the slow rise and fall of Nadia's shoulders as she lets her head fall against Varda's shoulder with a quiet, shaky sigh. The rigid, defensive armor of her posture finally collapses, her bound hands resting limp on her lap as she surrenders to the old woman’s embrace. In the Force, her presence is no longer a jagged, freezing needle of fury, but a soft, blue pool of sorrow, reflecting the ancient warmth of Varda's healing touch. 

She closes her eyes, a final, shuddering sigh escaping her lips, the tears finally tracking wet pathways through the grime on her cheeks. 

**"My mother used to... she used to hold me like this when the noise got too loud,"** Nadia whispers, her voice thick and small against the fabric of Varda's cardigan. **"She would tell me that the Force was a great, deep sea, and that I didn't need to fight the waves. I just had to float until the storm passed."** She leans deeper into the old woman’s embrace, the manacled links of her binders clicking softly. **"But the sea has been so cold since she left, Varda. I've felt like I’ve been drowning for two years."**

**"I'm so tired, Varda,"** Nadia whispers, her voice muffled against the fabric of the cardigan, shaking with the release of the storm. She remains still, her breathing slowly regulating as the physical warmth of Varda’s arm and the slow, steady hum of the ship’s engines act as a shield against the persistent, cold static in her mind. Her Force presence begins to soften, the raw edges of her anger rounding out into a quiet, resting ember.

Venture • Airlock

At the primary airlock, the scene is one of disciplined, militaristic chaos. A massive automated cargo sled hums as it drops off two crates of heavy-duty MREs and a secure container of dark, bitter caf. Surrounding the sled are Arno, Kella, Tor, and three other Skirata warriors in deep-crimson, sand-gold, and a dull, battle-scarred blue armor are methodically checking the status of their high-yield scatter-blasters and heavy vibro-blades.

Wes Del-Fin (<@352982222636187659>) Iyola Kett (<@626586611500711967>)

Varda’s second sight does not simply read Nadia’s surface emotions; it penetrates her spiritual landscape like a lantern revealing a complex, hand-woven tapestry. Varda sees Nadia’s soul as a warp and weft of contrasting threads—the deep blue of her Mandalorian pride, the soft, pale light of her early Jedi training. But the most recent section of this weave is violently shredded and torn, the fibers charred by fire and frayed by abuse; this is the raw, agonizing trauma of her recent captivity under Rask, dominating her immediate state. Just behind these ruined threads, however, Varda detects an unnaturally smooth, clean gap—a section where the weave has been carefully and cleanly sheared away by an external blade, leaving an empty void in her history. But deepest of all, buried far beneath the charred ruins and the empty void, Varda finds a dense knot of dark, iron-hard threads. It is bound so tightly into the very core of her soul that it feels less like a pattern and more like an immovable anchor. It remains quiet, hidden under the frayed, bleeding threads of her recent suffering, but if Varda’s mind even lightly touches this deep knot, it hums with a tense, warning vibration, suggesting that any attempt to unpick it would unravel the entire piece.


The heavy door slides open with a pneumatic sigh. 

An older woman of refined, timeless grace steps into the suite. Her soft grey hair isstyled in an elegant wave that frames a face of quiet intelligence. She wears flowing, detailed robes—a deep teal under-gown draped in a rich, burgundy cloak that wraps around her shoulders like a senatorial shawl. In one hand, she holds a simple, polished wooden staff, her bearing reflecting the educated, aristocratic pedigree of the Core Worlds rather than the utilitarian grime of the Outer Rim.

A silent, armored guard follows in her shadow, carrying a simple metal tray that holds a steaming, dark clay pot of tea and two cups.

Sindara stops a few paces inside the room, her grey-toned eyes studying the two occupants. Her gaze lingers on Varda’s upright posture and the gnarled staff in her hands, her expression one of polite, guarded curiosity. She gestures to the guard to set the tray on the low table between the cots. **"The air is cold on this deck,"** she says, her voice a calm, commanding rasp that carries the educated accent of one of the Core worlds. **"I find a pot of tea is more effective than standard environmental controls for settling the chill. I hope you'll join me."**

Nadia remains seated on the edge of the bed, her visorless face setting into a wary, protective mask as she looks from Sindara to the silent guard. She doesn't reach for the tea, her manacled wrists clicking softly as she shifts her weight, her blue eyes dark with an unblinking focus as she awaits Varda's lead.
